features.name: localization
features.description: Multi-language support allowing the application UI to be displayed in the user's preferred language with community-contributed translations.

flows.name: localize-ui-flow
flows.description: Set page locale and retrieve localization resources for multi-language UI support.

features.name: monitor
features.description: Core uptime monitoring feature allowing users to create, configure, and manage monitors for websites, services, and endpoints with condition-based alerting.

flows.name: create-monitor
flows.description: User fills out the monitor form to create a new monitor with type, settings, and alert conditions.

flows.name: edit-monitor
flows.description: User edits an existing monitor's configuration including type, general settings, and conditional alert rules.

flows.name: configure-monitor-conditions
flows.description: User adds, removes, or groups logical conditions (with AND/OR operators) that determine when a monitor triggers an alert.

flows.name: push-monitor-heartbeat
flows.description: Sends a push heartbeat to an Uptime Kuma push monitor endpoint using one of the provided language-specific example clients.

flows.name: monitor-url-resolution-flow
flows.description: Resolve relative URLs for monitor resources.

features.name: notifications
features.description: Notification system enabling users to configure and manage alert channels (email, Slack, PagerDuty, etc.) and receive alerts when monitors change state.

flows.name: manage-notifications
flows.description: User adds, edits, tests, or deletes notification channels for monitor alerts.

flows.name: configure-notification-provider
flows.description: User opens the notification dialog, selects a provider type, and fills in provider-specific credentials to create or update a notification channel.

flows.name: test-notification-provider
flows.description: User triggers a test dispatch to verify that a configured notification provider is reachable and working correctly.

flows.name: delete-notification-provider
flows.description: User confirms and permanently removes an existing notification provider configuration.

flows.name: send-whatsapp-alert-via-whapi
flows.description: User receives a monitor status notification sent as a WhatsApp message through the Whapi gateway.

bug_fix_prs.author: Copilot

features.name: status-pages
features.description: Public-facing status pages that display the health of selected monitors to external users, with incident management and RSS feed support.

flows.name: view-status-page
flows.description: Public user visits a status page by slug to see current monitor uptime and incidents.

flows.name: subscribe-status-page-rss
flows.description: Public user subscribes to a status page's incident feed via RSS.

flows.name: create-status-page
flows.description: Admin creates a new status page with a title and unique slug.

flows.name: configure-status-page
flows.description: Admin edits a status page's configuration, branding, and monitor group visibility.

flows.name: delete-status-page
flows.description: Admin permanently deletes a status page and its associated data.

flows.name: post-incident-update
flows.description: Admin creates or edits a pinned incident notice on a status page.

flows.name: resolve-incident
flows.description: Admin resolves or unpins an active incident on a status page.

flows.name: embed-status-badge
flows.description: User retrieves an SVG uptime badge for a monitor to embed externally.

features.name: devops-tooling
features.description: Internal developer and release tooling for building, deploying, versioning, and maintaining the Uptime Kuma project.

flows.name: publish-nightly-release
flows.description: Builds frontend dist and Docker images tagged as nightly for automated overnight releases.

flows.name: publish-beta-release
flows.description: Validates version format, builds Docker images, creates dist tarball and release PR for a beta version.

flows.name: publish-final-release
flows.description: Validates semver, builds and pushes Docker images, and creates the final production release PR.

flows.name: deploy-demo-server
flows.description: SSH into the demo server and interactively deploys a specified version by checking out, downloading dist, and installing dependencies.

flows.name: run-healthcheck
flows.description: Performs an HTTP health check against the application endpoint and exits with a status code indicating health.

flows.name: download-release-dist
flows.description: Downloads and extracts the pre-built frontend dist tarball for a given release version.

flows.name: release-packaging-flow
flows.description: Create distribution archives and manage release artifacts for deployment.

flows.name: release-pr-creation-flow
flows.description: Create pull requests for release management and versioning.

features.name: settings
features.description: Application-wide settings covering appearance, monitor history retention, proxy configuration, remote browsers, reverse proxy, tags, and general preferences.

flows.name: configure-general-settings
flows.description: User views and saves general application settings such as timezone, language, and monitor defaults.

flows.name: manage-proxies
flows.description: User adds, edits, or removes proxy servers used by monitors.

flows.name: manage-tags
flows.description: User creates, edits, or deletes tags used to categorize monitors.

flows.name: customize-appearance
flows.description: User adjusts UI theme, color scheme, and display preferences.

flows.name: configure-remote-browsers
flows.description: User sets up remote browser connections used for browser-based monitors.

features.name: maintenance
features.description: Maintenance window scheduling that suppresses alerts and marks monitors as under maintenance during planned downtime periods.

flows.name: create-maintenance-window
flows.description: User creates a new scheduled maintenance window with title, strategy, date/time range, and timezone.

flows.name: edit-maintenance-window
flows.description: User updates the settings of an existing maintenance window and re-runs its schedule.

flows.name: assign-monitors-to-maintenance
flows.description: User selects which monitors are affected by a given maintenance window.

flows.name: assign-status-pages-to-maintenance
flows.description: User links one or more status pages to a maintenance window so they display the maintenance notice.

flows.name: view-maintenance-details
flows.description: User fetches a single maintenance window or the full list, including its associated monitors and status pages.

flows.name: delete-maintenance-window
flows.description: User permanently removes a maintenance window and stops its scheduled execution.

flows.name: pause-resume-maintenance-window
flows.description: User pauses or resumes an active maintenance window to temporarily suspend or restore its scheduled effect.

bug_fix_prs.title: fix: MariaDB datetime format error when pausing maintenance (#6513)
bug_fix_prs.author: Copilot

bug_fix_prs.title: Fix: Maintenance: Handle optional startDateTime to prevent errors when null (#6189)
bug_fix_prs.author: Louis Lam

bug_fix_prs.title: Fix invalid maintenance date (#5901)
bug_fix_prs.author: Ionys

features.name: api-keys
features.description: API key management allowing users to generate, view, and revoke API keys for programmatic access to Uptime Kuma.

flows.name: generate-api-key
flows.description: User creates and stores a new API key for programmatic access to the monitoring API.

flows.name: manage-api-keys
flows.description: User creates or revokes API keys for programmatic access to Uptime Kuma.

features.name: server
features.description: Files under server/ extracted from status-pages (auto-split for granularity)

features.name: auth
features.description: Authentication and account access — sign-in, sign-up, password recovery, email verification, session management.

flows.name: reset-user-password
flows.description: Connects to a running Uptime Kuma instance via socket and resets a user's password from the command line.

flows.name: configure-security
flows.description: User changes their password, enables two-factor authentication, and adjusts authentication settings.
